summary: Introduces the Interior Observer framework: the observable universe lives inside a Schwarzschild black hole and the CMB is interior Hawking radiation. Version v4.1 adds the audited foundational theorem registry, including the finite horizon-readable quotient, total-energy/density accounting separation, native closed-S3 mode reduction, typed-observable guardrails, and Schwarzschild parent neutrality.
full_title: Paper 1 — A Black Hole Cosmology with the CMB as Event Horizon Radiation
title_tag: Paper 1 — A Black Hole Cosmology | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 1 v4.1 introduces the Interior Observer framework and adds the audited foundational theorem registry with closed-S3, typed-observable, and Schwarzschild-neutrality guardrails.

summary: Shows that semiclassical QFT on stationary Schwarzschild spacetime cannot generate the boost factor γ, then derives γ from the Carlip-Virasoro horizon algebra in the dimensionally reduced interior-observer setting. It also builds a holographic thermalization bridge from 1+1D horizon states to the observed 3+1D photon bath and characterizes the framework's space-time decoupling hierarchy.
short_form: Paper 2 — Deriving γ from Horizon Algebra
full_title: Paper 2 — Derivation of γ from Quantum Gravity, the Bogoliubov No-Go Theorem, the Holographic Thermalization Bridge, the Space-Time Decoupling Problem, and the Holographic Timescale Hierarchy
title_tag: Paper 2 — Deriving γ from Horizon Algebra | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Semiclassical QFT on Schwarzschild cannot supply the boost factor gamma; Paper 2 derives gamma instead from the Carlip-Virasoro horizon algebra.

summary: Derives the two-phase Vaidya-to-Oppenheimer-Snyder interior structure from Israel junction conditions with zero free parameters, including the observer coordinate and the total interior lifecycle. The paper also identifies the base thermal-scale invariant and proves the IO transfer function is analytic across the interior history.
full_title: Paper 3 — The Two-Phase Interior, the Cosmological Invariant, and the Observer Coordinate
title_tag: Paper 3 — The Two-Phase Interior Model | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Israel junction conditions derive the two-phase Vaidya-to-Oppenheimer-Snyder interior with zero free parameters, including the observer clock and crossing map.

summary: Collects the framework's first broad evidence packet by tying horizon-interior observables to zero-fit calculations: the base interior thermal scale, dark-energy density, MOND acceleration scale, and boost factor γ. It also confronts the model with galaxy-formation timing and early JWST tensions, emphasizing the extra formation time available on the IO expansion history.
full_title: Paper 4 — Evidence for a Black Hole Interior: Four Horizon Connections, JWST Predictions, and Quantitative Tensions
title_tag: Paper 4 — Four Horizon Connections Tested | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Early horizon-interior observables — base thermal scale, dark energy, age, and angular scale — are tested in the first IO evidence packet.

summary: Explores three early structural questions: how black-hole spin affects the interior thermal scale, whether the Vaidya radiation phase is compatible with an isotropic CMB bath, and what early-time interior physics is required. The analysis favors the Schwarzschild limit, rules out the naive Vaidya null-dust phase for CMB acoustics, and sharpens the framework's early-radiation picture.
short_form: Paper 5 — Kerr Limit and Radiation Phase
full_title: Paper 5 — The Kerr Limit, the Radiation Phase, and Early-Time Physics in Black Hole Cosmology
title_tag: Paper 5 — Kerr Limit and Radiation Phase | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 5 tests spin dependence, Vaidya compatibility, and early-time completeness, sharpening which early IO structures survive as viable theorem routes.

summary: Computes the full CMB TT spectrum with the CLASS Boltzmann code using the IO background geometry together with borrowed ΛCDM primordial inputs. The result is a clean geometric rescaling of the ΛCDM spectrum, with the acoustic peak structure preserved but shifted uniformly to larger angular scales, making the perturbation-sector gap explicit.
full_title: Paper 6 — The Full CMB Power Spectrum in Black Hole Cosmology: A CLASS Boltzmann Code Analysis
title_tag: Paper 6 — Full CMB Power Spectrum | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Computes the full CMB TT spectrum with the CLASS Boltzmann code using the IO background geometry together with borrowed ΛCDM primordial inputs.

summary: Applies the early IO cosmology to Big Bang nucleosynthesis and finds a mixed result: helium is viable, lithium is improved relative to ΛCDM, but deuterium is overproduced because of the low baryon-to-photon ratio. The paper also frames the resulting combined tension and introduces the c³ hypothesis as part of the early-stage response.
short_form: Paper 7 — BBN and the c³ Hypothesis
full_title: Paper 7 — Big Bang Nucleosynthesis, the Unified Tension, and the c³ Hypothesis
title_tag: Paper 7 — BBN and the c³ Hypothesis | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Early IO Big Bang nucleosynthesis gives viable helium, improved lithium, and a clear deuterium miss, establishing the first honest BBN boundary of the framework.

summary: Introduces the Rosetta Stone identity Δ = x⁴(1+γ²), which unifies the temporal decoupling, dark-energy normalization gap, and age tension into a single geometric relationship. The identity is numerically verified at high precision and becomes a central organizing formula for later papers.
full_title: Paper 8 — The Rosetta Stone Identity: Unifying Dark Energy, Temporal Decoupling, and Quantum Gravity in Black Hole Cosmology
title_tag: Paper 8 — The Rosetta Stone Identity | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: The Rosetta Stone identity Delta = x^4(1+gamma^2) unifies temporal decoupling, dark-energy normalization, and age tension into one exact relation.

summary: Closes the missing factor in the dark-energy derivation by identifying √(1+γ²) with the spectral-norm ratio of the Ashtekar-Barbero connection to the spin connection on the Schwarzschild horizon. The paper shows that the relevant connection pieces occupy orthogonal subspaces and that their spectral norms match exactly on the horizon slice used by the framework.

title_tag: Paper 9 — Gauge Projection for Dark Energy | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Gauge projection closes the dark-energy derivation by identifying the missing sqrt(1+gamma^2) factor with a connection-to-spin spectral norm ratio.

summary: Derives the complete IO Friedmann equation, combining the matter and bare-Λ sector with the energy projection √Δ and the curvature projection Δ^(-1/x²). The paper presents the framework's first full holographic background equation with zero free parameters and makes the modular-energy role of the projection factors explicit.
full_title: Paper 10 — The Complete Holographic Friedmann Equation — Zero Free Parameters, One Physical Principle

meta_description: A zero-parameter cosmological constant derivation from the holographic Friedmann equation predicts Ω_Λ = 0.697, +0.92σ from the Planck 2018 reference.

summary: Subjects the framework to an extended adversarial stress test against CMB perturbations, BAO absolute distances, and BBN. It derives the Schur-complement curvature formula, identifies a species-dependent baryon correction, and introduces a modular-projection idea that substantially improves the framework's observational viability relative to its earlier form.

summary: Responds to an internal kill-shot audit by replacing the ad hoc baryon fraction with the Baryon Dictionary Principle, which identifies baryons as the gauge-coupled subset of Oppenheimer-Snyder dust and fixes f_b = 2γ/x. The paper also introduces the discrete α-ladder and argues that α = 1 is the unique projection scaling consistent with the observed deuterium abundance.

summary: Introduces the Gauge Thermal Transfer Principle as the historical route to observer-side thermal readout. Current framework wording treats the observed CMB temperature as the FIRAS empirical datum that fixes the unique readout normalization, not as an independent IO prediction.

summary: Audits three major structural vulnerabilities: frame dependence of the spectral norm, the N_eff closure, and the H₀ projection chain. The paper sharpens the horizon-intrinsic interpretation of K_gauge, refines the transfer identification, and maps which parts of the framework survive rigorous adversarial checking.

summary: Derives the Rosetta identity structurally within the framework through three theorems: the Quaternionic Norm Theorem, the Half-Density Carrier Theorem, and a conformal-transgression step that supplies the x² factor. It also proves a boundary no-go theorem showing that the compactness ratio x cannot be reconstructed from local horizon data alone.

summary: Resolves the final degree question in the dark-energy derivation: why the gauge projection appears as √(1+γ²) rather than 1+γ². The paper develops a set of transfer-homogeneity and locality theorems, adds a composite-uniqueness argument, and narrows the allowed projection degree on the physical channel.

short_title: General Relativity and Quantum Mechanics Unified
summary: An event horizon speaks three quantum languages to the classical interior it contains. The Interior Observer (IO) framework models the observable universe as the interior of a Schwarzschild black hole. Across 32 papers, it derives or scopes the base thermal scale, dark energy density, baryon fraction, three BBN abundances (χ² = 1.13), the spectral index, the scalar amplitude, and the gravitational slip — all with explicit claim labels and no downstream retuning. This paper reveals the organizing principle: the boundary-to-bulk crossings fall into three channels. Channel 1 (Geometric) is always on — the boundary's gauge algebra permanently imprints on bulk geometry. Channel 2 (Thermal) is state-gated — processes in thermal equilibrium with the Hawking/KMS boundary state receive geometric dressing. Channel 3 (Fermionic) is density-gated — at Planck density, boundary spinor data sources the bulk spin fluid that drives the torsion bounce, with R_eq/R_bounce = 1.00016 from representation theory alone. Includes: a geometric dark-matter alternative (predicting continued null results from direct detection), a conditional no-go for quantum gravity signatures in the muon g−2, and the Holographic Selection Rule governing which observables see the boundary and which do not. Interactive bridge map: https://dfife.github.io/bridge-map.html

title_tag: Paper 33 — General Relativity and Quantum Mechanics Unified | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 33 reframes the archive as three horizon languages and packages 32 zero-parameter derivations linking general relativity, quantum mechanics, and cosmology.
schema_keywords: General Relativity and Quantum Mechanics Unified

summary: The Hubble tension — the 5σ discrepancy between the Planck CMB measurement (67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc) and the SH0ES Cepheid distance ladder (73.0 ± 1.0 km/s/Mpc) — is resolved with zero fitted parameters. Paper 34 v2.0 adds the scoped Planck compound-CMB classification, closes the §10 extension ledger within stated physically-admissible classes, and records the Paper 29 projected observer branch as the sole active baseline. Six methods fall within 0.57σ of prediction, with SH0ES at +0.04σ.
full_title: Paper 34 — The Hubble Tension Resolved: Six Methods, Three Rungs, Zero Fitted Parameters
title_tag: Paper 34 — Six Methods, Three Rungs, Zero Fitted Parameters | Interior Observer Framework
meta_description: Paper 34 v2.0 resolves the Hubble tension with six H0 methods within 0.57σ, adds scoped Planck and §10 closure theorems, and publishes the v2.0 reproducibility bundle.

summary: Paper 35 packages four confrontation layers on the current IO branch. It closes the late-time baryon-to-photon ratio route, sharpens the BBN bulk-frame η route, reduces baryogenesis to an exact topological obstruction on the current stack, shows that the IO master clock gives 46-48% more formation time than Planck ΛCDM at z > 10, corrects the observer-side dark-energy mapping away from the retired w = -1/3 carryover, and frames continuing direct-detection null results as the expected outcome of the geometric dark-sector reading.
